# Feature Ideas — 2026-04-17

**Generated by:** `/ideas` (PRODUCT IDEA GENERATOR profile)
**Scope:** Whole project, general feature list (no focus area specified)

---

## Project today

A SwiftUI iOS 17+ expense tracker with two features behind a `TabView`: **Add Expense** (title, amount, currency, optional category, date) and **Budget Planning** (monthly overall + per-category budgets with live spend tracking and progress bars). Architecture is strict MVVM + Repository + DataSource + UseCase.

## User & core job

A solo iOS user who wants to **log an expense in ≤5 seconds and see whether they're on track against a monthly plan** — without spreadsheets or complex apps like YNAB.

## Critical gap flagged up front

**No persistence.** `InMemoryExpenseDataSource` and `InMemoryBudgetDataSource` (both `actor`s) lose everything on app restart. Every idea below assumes this gets solved first or alongside — so **Idea #1 is that foundation**.

---

## Feature ideas (ranked by value × fit)

### 1. SwiftData persistence layer

- **Value** — Without this, the app literally forgets what the user typed. Every other feature inherits this requirement.
- **Effort** — **L**. Add `@Model Expense`, `@Model Budget`; introduce `SwiftDataExpenseDataSource` and `SwiftDataBudgetDataSource` as new `ExpenseDataSource` / `BudgetDataSource` conformers; swap in `AppContainer.init`. `Sendable` / `Codable` models already line up nicely.
- **Fit** — `Tracker/Features/AddExpense/Data/`, `Tracker/Features/Budget/Data/`, `Tracker/Core/AppContainer.swift`. Zero changes to ViewModels, UseCases, or Views — architecture already supports a cache/persistence layer swap.
- **Why now** — It's the single biggest believability gap. Ship anything else on top of in-memory storage and users churn on session #2.

### 2. Expense history view (see what you logged)

- **Value** — Today users can **add** expenses but have **no** way to view, edit, or delete them. This is the most glaring functional gap after persistence.
- **Effort** — **M**. New `Features/ExpenseList/` with `ExpenseListViewModel` (filters by `YearMonth`, category, currency), a list view, a detail/edit sheet reusing `AmountHeroField`, `CurrencyChip`, `CategoryPicker`. `ExpenseRepository` already has `expenses(in:calendar:)` — needs `update(_:)` and `delete(id:)` additions.
- **Fit** — Adds a third tab to `ContentView.swift`; extends `ExpenseRepository`; reuses the entire design system.
- **Why now** — Completes the CRUD loop — prerequisite for every insight, trend, or export idea below.

### 3. Spending insights dashboard (the "aha" screen)

- **Value** — Turns raw logs into decisions: "Where did February's money go?" with a category donut + a 6-month trend line.
- **Effort** — **M**. New `GetSpendingBreakdownUseCase` (reuses `expenses(in:)` × 6 months), a `SpendingViewModel`, and a view using `Charts` (iOS 17 native). No new data required — it's pure analytics over existing `Expense`.
- **Fit** — New feature dir `Features/Insights/`; sits next to Budget. `BudgetSummary.categoryBreakdowns` is already the right shape — extend it into a multi-month series.
- **Why now** — Once persistence + history exist, this is the lightest-touch, highest-perceived-value addition. It's also what makes the user come back between expense logs.

### 4. Recurring expenses (subscriptions, rent)

- **Value** — Users don't want to re-enter Spotify every month. Auto-logging recurring charges also makes budgets drastically more accurate.
- **Effort** — **M**. New `RecurringExpense` model (`Expense` + `Recurrence` enum: `.weekly` / `.monthly` / `.yearly` + `nextDueDate`); `RecurringExpenseRepository`; a `MaterializeRecurringExpensesUseCase` run on app launch / tab focus that creates concrete `Expense` entries for dates that have arrived.
- **Fit** — New feature dir. Reuses `Expense`, `Currency`, `ExpenseCategory`. Hooks into `AppContainer` for startup materialization.
- **Why now** — Natural extension once you can edit expenses (Idea #2). Also meaningfully improves Budget accuracy — a core loop feature that pays off other features.

### 5. Budget-threshold notifications

- **Value** — Classic retention hook. "You've hit 80% of your Coffee budget" brings users back at exactly the right moment.
- **Effort** — **S**. After each `submit()` in `AddExpenseViewModel` call a new `CheckBudgetThresholdUseCase(expense: Expense) → BudgetAlert?` that re-runs `GetMonthlyBudgetSummaryUseCase` and fires a `UNUserNotificationCenter` local notification at configurable thresholds (50/80/100%).
- **Fit** — New `Features/Notifications/` with a `NotificationService` (`@MainActor` facade, side-effect isolation); use case sits in `Features/Budget/UseCases/`. Zero Data layer changes.
- **Why now** — Cheapest retention feature available; requires nothing beyond Ideas #1 and UseCase already written.

### 6. CSV / PDF export

- **Value** — Tax time, partner sharing, spreadsheet power users. Also reduces lock-in fear — ironically *increases* retention.
- **Effort** — **S**. `ExportMonthUseCase(yearMonth: YearMonth, format: .csv | .pdf) → URL`. CSV is trivial string building; PDF via `ImageRenderer` over a hidden SwiftUI view. Trigger from the Budget screen toolbar.
- **Fit** — Pure read-side feature; new `Features/Export/`. Uses `ExpenseRepository.expenses(in:)` and existing `MoneyFormatter`. `.fileExporter` modifier handles the share sheet.
- **Why now** — Once you have a few months of data (Idea #1 + #4), this becomes instantly valuable. Low effort, high perceived completeness.

### 7. Home Screen / Lock Screen widget + Siri quick-add

- **Value** — Friction kills expense trackers. A widget that shows "this month: $247 of $500" + a Siri shortcut "Add $4 coffee" makes logging almost invisible.
- **Effort** — **M**. Widget extension target reads summary via a `UserDefaults`-backed `BudgetSummaryCache` that `BudgetPlanningViewModel` writes after each load. App Intent (`AppIntent` protocol, iOS 16+) for Siri wraps the existing `ExpenseRepository.add(_:)`.
- **Fit** — New targets (Widget Extension + Intents); new `SharedModels` layer needed (extract core models out — they're already `Sendable` and `Codable`).
- **Why now** — Differentiates the app from "yet another form-based tracker." Makes Idea #2's data visible without opening the app.

### 8. Biometric lock (Settings screen)

- **Value** — Expense data is private. Face ID on launch is table-stakes for a finance app.
- **Effort** — **S**. `LocalAuthentication` in an `AuthService`; an `@MainActor AppLockViewModel` gates `ContentView`; toggle in a new minimal `SettingsView`.
- **Fit** — Root-level wrapper around `ContentView`. New `Features/Settings/`.
- **Why now** — Quick win, privacy signal, unblocks App Store review objections about finance apps.

---

## Quick wins

Deliverable in ≤1 day each once Idea #1 (persistence) is in:

- **#5 Budget-threshold notifications** — reuses the existing summary use case, just adds one service.
- **#6 CSV export** — `ImageRenderer` / string building + `.fileExporter`. No new models.
- **#8 Biometric lock** — one service + one settings toggle.

## Bigger bets

Worth a roadmap milestone each:

- **#1 SwiftData persistence** — foundational; rest of roadmap depends on it.
- **#2 Expense history / CRUD** — closes the core loop.
- **#3 Insights dashboard** — the "why you opened the app today" screen.
- **#4 Recurring expenses** — makes budgets believable.
- **#7 Widget + Siri quick-add** — differentiates and reduces friction to near zero.

## Out of scope for now (tempting but premature)

- **Bank sync / Plaid integration** — regulatory + onboarding burden too high for pre-persistence app.
- **Multi-currency auto-conversion with live FX rates** — the VM already scopes one currency at a time via `GetMonthlyBudgetSummaryUseCase`; adding FX adds a network dependency against CLAUDE.md's "no networking in ViewModels" discipline without a clear offline story yet.
- **Shared household budgets / multi-user** — needs CloudKit or a backend; no auth model exists.
- **ML-based category suggestions from title text** — worth revisiting once Idea #2 gives us a corpus of titles→categories to learn from.
- **Subscription monetization** — the app hasn't demonstrated retention yet; build Ideas #3 and #5 first to see if users come back.

---

## Suggested sequencing

```
Phase 0 (foundation)  : #1 SwiftData persistence
Phase 1 (core loop)   : #2 Expense history  +  #8 Biometric lock
Phase 2 (retention)   : #5 Notifications    +  #4 Recurring expenses
Phase 3 (insight)     : #3 Insights dashboard +  #6 Export
Phase 4 (differentiate): #7 Widget + Siri quick-add
```
